seo.og_title: Better Auth — Pricing, Developer Experience & Best Use Cases
seo.og_description: Better Auth for Next.js App Router: Medium learning curve, Free (Open Source), 30 minutes setup. Open-source control, zero vendor lock-in, plugin architecture for custom auth flows

hero.headline: Better Auth: Complete Review for Next.js Developers
hero.subheadline: A thorough breakdown of Better Auth's pricing, developer experience, and fit for Next.js App Router projects.

intro_paragraphs: Better Auth is a medium authentication solution that is completely free and open source. It is designed for Next.js App Router and supports authentication patterns like fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage limits and self-hosted on your own infrastructure - complete data control. Estimated setup time is 30 minutes for a basic integration.
who_should_use_paragraphs: You should consider Better Auth if you prioritize fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage. It is well-suited for teams that need a reliable Next.js auth provider and want to avoid vendor lock-in or excessive per-user pricing.
pricing_context: Better Auth itself is free and open source, but 'free' here is misleading when you factor in infrastructure costs. You need a database (Postgres, MySQL, or SQLite), a server or serverless functions to host the API routes, and ongoing maintenance for security patches and updates. For a production Next.js app, you are looking at $20-50/mo in hosting costs plus engineering time for setup and maintenance. While there is no per-user pricing, the operational cost can rival or exceed managed auth services for small teams. Auth.js offers a similar open-source model with a larger community and more battle-tested codebase.

faq.question: Is Better Auth good for Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Better Auth has a Next.js integration score of 8/10. Better Auth takes a framework-first approach with deep Next. Better Auth is one of the best options available for App Router projects.

faq.answer: Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source) with Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. Better Auth itself is free and open source, but 'free' here is misleading when you factor in infrastructure costs. Factor in your expected MAU when evaluating total cost.
